# GitHub Discussions Guidelines for DSA Handbook

This document explains the recommended Discussion categories and contains a ready-to-post **Announcement** text.

## Recommended Discussion Categories

When setting up Discussions in the repo (`Settings → Discussions → Categories`), create these six categories:

| Category              | Type              | What it's for                                                                 | Recommended Settings          |
|-----------------------|-------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------|
| **Announcements**     | Announcements     | Official updates from maintainers only (new chapters, site changes, releases) | **Pinned**, Moderators only   |
| **Q&A**               | Q&A               | Questions and help about any data structure, algorithm, or example code       | Default category              |
| **Ideas & Feedback**  | Ideas             | Suggestions for new topics, better explanations, new examples, or site features | -                             |
| **Show & Tell**       | Show and tell     | Share your implementations, learning progress, visualizations, or projects    | -                             |
| **Resources**         | General           | Share external articles, videos, papers, tools, or related learning material  | -                             |
| **General**           | General           | Broad or off-topic conversations related to DSA learning                      | -                             |

**Why these categories?**
- Keeps the space organized and useful for learners.
- `Announcements` gives high visibility to important updates.
- `Q&A` and `Ideas` are the most active for community engagement.
- `Show & Tell` builds motivation and showcases real usage.

---

## Ready-to-Post Announcement (Copy & Paste)

Post this as the **first discussion** in the **Announcements** category, then **pin** it.

```markdown
# 👋 Welcome to the DSA Handbook Discussions!

Hello everyone!

This is the official community space for the **[DSA Handbook](https://aryagorjipour.github.io/dsa)** — a complete, practical, and no-nonsense guide to Data Structures & Algorithms.

### What makes this handbook special
- Full, production-style implementations in **C#** and **Go**
- Every topic includes a real motivating problem (not just theory)
- Real-world context (databases, systems, interviews, etc.)
- 35+ Data Structures and 48 Algorithms with detailed explanations

**Live website**: https://aryagorjipour.github.io/dsa

### Companion Project
If you enjoy learning through play, check out **[Structura](https://aryagorjipour.github.io/structura/)** — a dark fantasy RPG where you master the same concepts by fighting algorithmic bosses using quizzes, visualizations, and real code.

GitHub: https://github.com/Aryagorjipour/structura

### How These Discussions Are Organized

| Category             | What it's for |
|----------------------|---------------|
| **Announcements**    | Major updates from the maintainers (pinned) |
| **Q&A**              | Get help with specific topics or code |
| **Ideas & Feedback** | Suggest new content, improvements, or features |
| **Show & Tell**      | Share your implementations, learning progress, or projects |
| **Resources**        | Share great external articles, videos, or tools |
| **General**          | Broad or off-topic DSA-related discussion |

### Get Involved
- Stuck on something? → Ask in **Q&A**
- Have an idea? → Post in **Ideas & Feedback**
- Built something cool? → Share in **Show & Tell**

We're building one of the best free, practical DSA resources available. Your questions and contributions make it better for everyone.

Happy learning!

— [Aryagorjipour](https://github.com/Aryagorjipour)
```

---

**After posting:**
1. Pin the announcement.
2. (Optional) Set it as the repository welcome message in repo settings.

This gives the project strong initial visibility and clearly explains the purpose of each category.
